senses.definition: to manage to proceed as planned or produce the desired result

examples.x: After months of hard work and perseverance, everything fell into place for James, and he achieved his dream of starting his own business.
Despite initial setbacks, Susan's dedication and perseverance allowed things to fall into place, leading her to achieve her fitness goals.

senses.definition: to produce a satisfactory or successful result

examples.x: Despite facing initial setbacks, the team's perseverance eventually bore fruit when they won the championship.
The investment in the company's research and development department is expected to bear fruit in the form of groundbreaking innovations.
The long hours of studying and preparation for the exam finally bore fruit when Rebecca received the highest score in her class.

senses.definition: a thing that someone takes pride in, such as an accomplishment, honor, etc.

examples.x: After publishing her second book, it was another feather in her cap as an accomplished author.
Being selected as the team captain was a feather in his cap, reflecting his leadership skills and dedication to the sport.

senses.definition: to produce great results or be extremely successful

examples.x: Jane always goes to town on her work, consistently producing high-quality results.
The team had been preparing for the big game for months, and when they stepped onto the field, they went to town, scoring goal after goal and securing a decisive victory.

senses.definition: experiencing a period of great success or good luck

examples.x: Despite the pandemic, the online retailer remained on a tear, experiencing record sales and expanding their product offerings to meet the changing needs of customers.
Last month, the artist was on a tear, completing several new paintings and receiving multiple commissions.
Last year, the athlete was on a roll, winning several major competitions and setting new personal records.

word_list.pronunciation: ɑːnðə kɹˈɛst ʌv ðɪ ɐ wˈeɪv

senses.definition: used to refer to a point in which someone has achieved massive success

examples.x: After winning the Nobel Prize, Dr. Lee is on the crest of the wave in his field of study.
The company is on the crest of the wave following the successful launch of their new product line.
The singer is on the crest of a wave, having just won a Grammy award and receiving widespread acclaim for their latest album.

senses.definition: to be successful or experiencing a period of success

examples.x: Mark is currently riding high after receiving a promotion at work.
The actor rode high on his Oscar win, receiving many offers for new movie roles.

senses.definition: in a grand or impressive manner, often used to describe the successful start of an event, party, or project

examples.x: The party ended with a bang, with fireworks lighting up the sky as the clock struck midnight.
The product launch went out with a bang, with a viral social media campaign and record-breaking sales numbers.

senses.definition: used to convey that something is guaranteed to be accomplished

examples.x: The actor's Oscar win was in the bag after receiving widespread critical acclaim for their performance.
The team's victory was in the bag as soon as their star player scored the winning goal.
After acing all the practice tests, she felt that success in the exam was in the bag.
With their significant lead, victory in the game seemed to be in the bag for the home team.

senses.definition: to perform or entertain in a way that causes the audience to react with extreme enthusiasm, such as by applauding loudly or cheering

examples.x: The magician brought the house down with their amazing tricks, leaving the audience gasping in amazement.
The singer always brings the house down with her powerful voice and stage presence.
Their dance routine brought the house down at the competition.
The surprise guest appearance brought the house down and excited the crowd.
Her heartfelt acceptance speech brought the house down.

senses.definition: a thing that is considered someone's or something's greatest achievement

examples.x: Her latest novel is her crowning glory, receiving widespread critical acclaim and topping the bestseller lists.
The completion of the landmark building was the architect's crowning glory, showcasing their visionary design and construction skills.

senses.definition: the time when something is at its best or most successful

examples.x: The athlete is enjoying his salad days, achieving record-breaking performances and winning multiple championships.
The company's salad days were in the early years when they secured several major contracts and grew rapidly.
The fashion designer is currently enjoying her salad days, receiving prestigious awards and recognition for her unique and innovative designs.

word_list.pronunciation: hˈɪtʃ wˈʌnz wˈæɡən tʊ ɐ stˈɑːɹ

senses.definition: to become partners with or rely on someone who is already successful so that one can achieve the same level of success

examples.x: The successful CEO hitched her wagon to a star early in her career by partnering with a well-known industry leader.
The writer hoped to hitch her wagon to a star by submitting her manuscript to a top publishing house.

senses.definition: to become exceedingly successful

examples.x: The athlete won several consecutive championships and did not look back on their way to becoming a legend in their sport.
The entrepreneur plans to launch a new product and not look back as they grow their business into a global brand.

senses.definition: to be in a position where one's success is guaranteed

examples.x: After working hard for years, the successful business owner had got it made and could finally enjoy the rewards of their efforts.
The talented athlete has it made with a lucrative contract and sponsorships that guarantee their financial security.

senses.definition: to set very high goals and work hard to achieve them, even if they seem very difficult or impossible to reach

examples.x: The athlete is shooting for the stars by training hard and aiming for a spot on the national team.
The entrepreneur shot for the moon by pursuing a risky business venture that had the potential for huge rewards.
The startup company is reaching for the moon with its ambitious goals to revolutionize the industry.
The talented musician decided to reach for the stars by auditioning for a popular singing competition.

senses.definition: to achieve success or victory in a situation where it was not expected

examples.x: Despite facing many challenges, the young entrepreneur came up trumps and secured a major investment for their startup.
In the future, if she continues to work hard, the talented musician could come up trumps and win a Grammy award.

senses.definition: to function successfully, often in an unexpected or impressive way

examples.x: His speech worked like a charm, winning everyone over.
The repair worked like magic, and the machine was running smoothly again.
The system was designed to work like a charm, and it did.
The new strategy worked like magic, improving sales almost overnight.
Her idea worked like a charm, and the project was completed ahead of schedule.
The remedy worked like magic, and he felt better immediately.

senses.definition: a series of three victories, successes, or related accomplishments achieved by the same individual

examples.x: He won three Pulitzer Prizes in a row, achieving a literary hat trick.
The musician released a hat trick of hit songs, all of which topped the charts and became fan favorites.

senses.definition: to achieve quick and remarkable success or popularity

examples.x: He has risen like a rocket in his career, but if he doesn't perform well in his new position, he could fall like a stick.
If their new movie is a hit, their fame will rise like a rocket, but if it flops, it will fall like a stick.
The company's stock price rose like a rocket when they announced their new product, but it fell like a stick when it didn't meet expectations.
The new fashion trend rose like a rocket, but it's expected to fall like a stick soon.
Their popularity is rising like a rocket, but if they don't release a new album soon, it could fall like a stick.
